verses.1: Better is a dry morsel with quietness,
verses.2: A servant who deals wisely will rule over a son who causes shame,
verses.3: The refining pot is for silver, and the furnace for gold,
verses.5: Whoever mocks the poor reproaches his Maker.
verses.6: Children’s children are the crown of old men;
verses.7: Excellent speech isn’t fitting for a fool,
verses.8: A bribe is a precious stone in the eyes of him who gives it;
verses.9: He who covers an offense promotes love;
verses.10: A rebuke enters deeper into one who has understanding
verses.11: An evil man seeks only rebellion;
verses.12: Let a bear robbed of her cubs meet a man,
verses.14: The beginning of strife is like breaching a dam,
verses.15: He who justifies the wicked, and he who condemns the righteous,
verses.16: Why is there money in the hand of a fool to buy wisdom,
verses.17: A friend loves at all times;
verses.18: A man void of understanding strikes hands,
verses.19: He who loves disobedience loves strife.
verses.20: One who has a perverse heart doesn’t find prosperity,
verses.21: He who becomes the father of a fool grieves.
verses.22: A cheerful heart makes good medicine,
verses.23: A wicked man receives a bribe in secret,
verses.24: Wisdom is before the face of one who has understanding,
verses.25: A foolish son brings grief to his father,
verses.26: Also to punish the righteous is not good,
verses.27: He who spares his words has knowledge.
verses.28: Even a fool, when he keeps silent, is counted wise.
